user_prompt: I want to write a `lib\FlyGui\GuiDisplay.cpp` and `lib\FlyGui\GuiDisplay.h`, implement a class `GuiDisplay`
Right now, we have a ton of places where `thefly_display` is used, but it is declared as a `M5GFX&` right now.
I need it to become a `GuiDisplay&` instead. Change none of the code that, hopefully just swap the class type and the rest of the code will just adapt, that's the goal.

Do this by implementing `GuiDisplay` such that it owns its own instance of `M5.Display` and then implementing a wrapper for all of the calls that `M5GFX` implements. `GuiDisplay` also owns a `M5Canvas` that is initialized to the same instance of `M5.Display`. The wrapper writes to both the `M5.Display` directly and to the `M5Canvas` simultaneously.

There will be a public `requestScreenshot` function that sets a flag. Then there will be a function, that when called, and if the flag is true, it saves the contents of the `M5Canvas` to a bitmap file on the microSD card, using the current date and time as the file name, and it clears that flag.

At the top of `void FlyGui::poll()`, if the `Serial.available()` indicates a character is available to be read, and then the character is a `s` keystroke, then call `requestScreenshot` to set the flag.
Near the end of `void FlyGui::poll()` where `drawn` is true, that's where the screenshot is saved.

Inside `inc\conf.h` I have added `TEST_BUILD_SCREENSHOT`. The screenshot keystroke check, the actual screenshot taking, and the actual existence of a real `GuiDisplay` class depends on this compile option. If the option is not defined, then `GuiDisplay` is an alias to `M5GFX`
agent_reply: It now conditionally becomes a real mirrored display under `TEST_BUILD_SCREENSHOT`, otherwise it aliases straight to `M5GFX`: [GuiDisplay.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/GuiDisplay.h:16), [GuiDisplay.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/GuiDisplay.h:397). The wrapper mirrors the current `thefly_display` calls to both `M5.Display` and an internal `M5Canvas`, including the `SpriteDraw` low-level `setAddrWindow/writePixels` path.

Wired `thefly_display` to `GuiDisplay&` in [Display.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/Display.h:5) and [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:89), and changed `FlyGui::display()` accordingly. `FlyGui::poll()` now watches serial for `s`/`S`, requests a screenshot, and saves it after the next successful draw: [FlyGui.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:140), [FlyGui.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:192). Screenshots are written as BMPs under `/screenshots` on the microSD using RTC date/time plus milliseconds: [GuiDisplay.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/GuiDisplay.cpp:46).
Result: `SUCCESS`. Note: I left the existing unrelated working-tree changes alone (`inc/conf.h`, `src/all_init.cpp`, `src/version.c`, plus existing untracked dirs).

user_prompt: move this highlighted chunk of code to one function call within `GuiDisplay.cpp`
agent_reply: Done. The serial `s`/`S` screenshot hotkey logic now lives in `GuiDisplay::pollScreenshotRequest()` in [GuiDisplay.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/GuiDisplay.cpp:41).
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec2 -j 2`: build succeeded.
Done. The serial `s`/`S` screenshot hotkey logic now lives in `GuiDisplay::pollScreenshotRequest()` in [GuiDisplay.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/GuiDisplay.cpp:41).
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec2 -j 2`: build succeeded.

user_prompt: can you please add another buffer, when the gui->poll ends and the screenshot is requested, copy the canvas to that buffer. the buffer can be overwritten by another screenshot request, it is not likely I will need a screenshot that frequently though.
and when a recording is not active, save that new buffer to file if needed
agent_reply: Done. `GuiDisplay` now owns a second `M5Canvas` snapshot buffer. At the end of `FlyGui::poll()`, `saveScreenshotIfRequested()` runs every completed GUI poll, not only when `drawn` is true.
- End of GUI poll copies the mirrored display canvas into `screenshotCanvas_` with a RAM `memcpy`.
- If recording is active, it keeps that copied screenshot pending and does no SD write.
- If another screenshot is requested before saving, it overwrites that pending buffer.
- Once `AudioFileRecorder::isRecording()` is false, it writes the buffered screenshot BMP to microSD.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec2 -j 2`: build succeeded. Small note: this adds one extra full-screen sprite buffer, so at 320x240x16-bit it costs about 150 KB of PSRAM.

commit_message: screenshot now works without disturbing recording
